text.source_text: कथं पुनस्तेजोबन्नानां त्रैरूप्येण त्रिरूपा अजा प्रतिपत्तुं शक्यते , यावता न तावत्तेजोबन्नेष्वजाकृतिरस्ति , न च तेजोबन्नानां जातिश्रवणादजातिनिमित्तोऽप्यजाशब्दः सम्भवतीति ; अत उत्तरं पठति —
text.transliteration_iast: kathaṃ punastejobannānāṃ trairūpyeṇa trirūpā ajā pratipattuṃ śakyate , yāvatā na tāvattejobanneṣvajākṛtirasti , na ca tejobannānāṃ jātiśravaṇādajātinimitto'pyajāśabdaḥ sambhavatīti ; ata uttaraṃ paṭhati —

translation.literal_translation: How can the threefold aja be understood through the threefoldness of fire, water, and food, when those elements do not literally have goat-form, and when the word aja cannot arise from hearing a species-name for those elements? To this, the answer is now read.
translation.technical_translation: Leading objection before sutra 1.4.10: challenges literal eligibility of aja-predication for the elemental triad, forcing an account of non-literal semantic transfer.
translation.interpretive_note: Sets up the kalpana-upadesa solution by first exhausting literal options.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: This block functions as a transition-objection rather than full doctrinal statement.
Terminological choice around aja is intentionally left neutral pending your final glossary lock.
